items.title: Wheelchair user calls for 'common sense' after fine threat for using accessible zone without permit

children.content: Disability advocate and wheelchair user Shane Hryhorec is calling for Adelaide Airport to use a common sense approach after being threatened with a fine for using an accessible pick-up zone without a permit.

gallery.altText: A man in a wheelchair with his service dog on his lap

thumbnail.altText: A man in a wheelchair with his service dog on his lap

items.title: Rankine sparks Crows with magical goals as Saints pump Eagles by 101 points

children.content: Izak Rankine shows his class in the first quarter as Adelaide battles Brisbane at the Gabba, while St Kilda destroys West Coast with a classy performance at Docklands. Follow live.

image.altText: Izak Rankine celebrates a goal with both arms raised

items.title: Police say body found on Fleurieu Peninsula was murder victim

children.content: SA Police say initial investigations suggest a person whose body has been found in bushland south of Adelaide was murdered.

image.altText: Two police officers walk on a steeply sloped dirt road among trees

items.title: This plant is the only known wild specimen in the world, found on just 300m of roadside

children.content: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

image.altText: The small fuzzy white flower of the Woods Well Spyridium

items.title: The donkey marching for the memory of all animals that serve in war

children.content: Penny the donkey, adorned in bright purple poppies, is spreading the Anzac Day legacy not just in Australia, but also in Cheshire in England.

image.altText: A grey donkey face up close in a paddock

items.title: Bitumen standards lowered after supply caught in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Drivers could soon be travelling on roads built for US and European climates, as supply disruptions force governments to change the standard of bitumen used in Australia.

gallery.altText: A road in the sunset, the flinders rangers can be seen in the distance

thumbnail.altText: A road in the sunset, the flinders rangers can be seen in the distance

avatar.alt: A young woman with brown hair smiles.

items.title: ICAC investigating senior SA police officer for alleged misconduct

children.content: The state's anti-corruption agency says the officer, who cannot be named, is under investigation to determine whether they "committed a breach of discipline contrary to the SA Police Code of Conduct".

image.altText: A building with a piping shrike logo on it

items.title: Woman chooses to go blind in one eye after delays in care

children.content: A 47-year-old woman receiving care at a public eye clinic in South Australia says her experience has been so poor she is choosing to go blind in one eye.

image.altText: A woman with blue eyes and short blonde hair smiles. She is outside.

items.title: 108yo and 103yo WWII veterans meet after more than 80 years

children.content: Two of Australia's oldest veterans served in the same place at the same time during World War II, and after the fighting, both headed home to resume their lives in SA — but the 108-year-old and the 103-year-old had never met until recently.

gallery.altText: Two elderly men seated at a table set for a lunch with a paper program

thumbnail.altText: Two elderly men seated at a table set for a lunch with a paper program

items.title: Bail denied for woman accused of repeatedly trying to kill ex-partner

children.content: The accused's lawyers applied for her release on bail last year on the basis that she had "dental and medical issues" that could not be managed in prison.

image.altText: A woman sitting at a desk in front of a bookcase

items.title: Councillor convicted of drink driving still able to attend Anzac Day parade

children.content: Berri Barmera Councillor Ian Schlein will hand in his licence several days after a regional court convicted him of drink driving, to allow him to attend an Anzac Day parade.

image.altText: a man smiles at the camera

items.title: Oyster growers enduring 'death by a thousand cuts' after being closed again

children.content: Oyster growing areas on the Yorke Peninsula are ordered to close due to the detection of a toxic phytoplankton for the second time this year.

gallery.altText: A man standing waist deep in water

thumbnail.altText: A man standing waist deep in water

items.title: New Adelaide Writers' Week boss says books, not political views, will inform line-up

children.content: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

image.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

items.title: Concerns community cohesion is 'going out the door' after Anzac booing

children.content: Jeers could be heard for more than a minute as Uncle Ray Minniecon spoke at yesterday's Anzac service in Sydney with similar scenes playing out in Melbourne and Perth.

image.altText: a group of young indigenous males march in redfern at the coloured diggers ceremony for anzac day 2026

items.title: Inside Chernobyl is a toxic world where frogs have turned black

children.content: Nuclear meltdown in 1986 reshaped ecosystems and human lives. Four decades on the legacy of the Chernobyl disaster is still unfolding.

image.altText: Two brown eyes with yellow and black nuclear symbols painted around them

items.title: Trump cancels Pakistan trip for US envoys as peace hopes fade

children.content: Donald Trump cancels a trip to Pakistan by US representatives Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with talks aimed at ending the two-month Iran war at a standstill as both Tehran and Washington show little willingness to soften their terms.

items.title: Prince Harry's comments about the US may loom over Charles's visit

children.content: Little more than a week after Meghan and Harry's "quasi royal" tour in Australia, King Charles will be conducting the real thing in America, with much higher stakes.

items.title: Thousands of Dutch families adopted the graves of people they never knew

children.content: In The Netherlands, a quiet commitment to honour the young soldiers who were buried thousands of kilometres from home has continued for 80 years.

image.altText: illustration of man and girl under red umberella looking at graves with dutch windmill and poppies

items.title: Married couple retire in their 90s after running small town's only hardware store for decades

children.content: The Pickles have been running the only hardware store in Junee for six decades, but now they are handing over the keys.

items.title: Act before commodities boom leaves us bust, urges former Treasury boss

children.content: Former Treasury secretary Ken Henry says the under-taxed resources sector has hollowed out Australia's economy, and raising more revenue from the sale of LNG resources was in the national interest.

image.altText: A man in a suit and white shirt stares into the distance

items.title: Dingo destroyed after week of attacks in WA's north-west

children.content: A male dingo has been "humanely destroyed" after multiple attacks within days of each other at Western Australia's famous Karijini National Park.

image.altText: A dingo runs along a park fence, surrounded by yellow and green scrub.

items.title: What Ringo did after the Beatles stunned the music world and he's still doing it 56 years later

children.content: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

items.title: Shaun Micallef would hate to be immortal, so he wrote a vampire novel

children.content: After Shaun Micallef finished up 11 years hosting comedy news show Mad as Hell in 2022, the comedian did something unexpected: he wrote a 'gothic romance novel'.

items.title: Sex workers speak out about being caught in the middle of PNG's HIV crisis

children.content: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.
